steps.title: Complete the square
steps.content:
Group the $x$ terms and complete the square to put the circle in standard form. Half of $62$ is $31$, and $31^2=961$:
$$x^2+62x+y^2=0$$
$$x^2+62x+961+y^2=961$$

steps.title: Read the center
steps.content:
Rewrite the equation in circle form and compare it with the standard form for a center point:
$$(x+31)^2+y^2=961$$This is $$(x-(-31))^2+(y-0)^2=31^2$$so the center is $(-31,0)$. The answer is D.
